scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement stipulated that the sale deed was to be executed on or before 10.07.2024. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

5. Pursuant to the said agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The Plaintiff has duly performed this obligation under the contract. A copy of the bank receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

6. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has arranged the necessary funds to discharge this liability and remains capable of paying the balance amount upon demand.

7. The Plaintiff has continuously maintained readiness to perform her part of the contract since the date of the agreement. The Plaintiff has kept the balance consideration ready and available for payment to the Defendant upon execution of the sale deed.

8. The possession of the suit property is {{POSSESSION_STATUS}}. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed in terms of the agreement. A copy of the legal notice with postal receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. On 05.06.2024, prior to the date fixed for execution, the Defendant communicated his inability to proceed with the transaction, citing receipt of a higher offer from a third party. A copy of the Defendant's correspondence is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

10. Despite the Plaintiff's demand via legal notice dated 20.06.2024, the Defendant failed to execute the sale deed on the agreed date of 10.07.2024. The Defendant has remained in default of his contractual obligation to transfer the title of the suit property to the Plaintiff.

11. The Defendant has not complied with the legal notice issued by the Plaintiff and has expressly refused to complete the sale. The Defendant's failure to perform constitutes a breach of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.

RELIEF CLAIMED

12. The Plaintiff seeks enforcement of the contract as the subject matter is such that monetary compensation would not afford adequate relief. The Plaintiff is entitled to have the agreement specifically performed.

13. The Plaintiff claims the value of the suit as Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee, representing the consideration agreed under the contract for specific performance.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

15. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

17. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
(b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
(c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Order XX Rule 11 CPC and Section 34 CPC respectively;
(e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

SCHEDULE OF SUIT PROPERTY

{{SCHEDULE_OF_SUIT_PROPERTY -- Address, Survey/Plot No., Extent/Area, Boundaries (East/West/North/South)}}

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 19 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. The Plaintiff states that {{MEDIATION_STATUS}}.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A true copy of the said Agreement is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789. A true copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed herewith as Annexure B.

8. Under the express terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024. The Plaintiff duly performed all obligations required on its part under the Agreement, including the payment of the agreed advance.

9. Upon noticing the default, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to perform its obligations or refund the advance. A true copy of the said legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure E.

10. The Plaintiff took steps to mitigate the loss arising from the non-delivery, including {{MITIGATION_STEPS}}. Despite such efforts, the Plaintiff incurred losses due to the Defendant's failure to perform.

BREACH PARTICULARS

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024 and remained in default thereafter.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the development or delivering any module. Email correspondence evidencing such abandonment is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

13. Despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the Defendant did not comply with the notice, nor did it refund the advance amount paid by the Plaintiff.

DAMAGES

14. As a direct consequence of the breach, the Plaintiff is entitled to a refund of the advance payment amounting to Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant.

15. The Plaintiff further suffered consequential damages amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by the non-delivery of the ERP system. The loss assessment report prepared by the auditor quantifying such loss is annexed herewith as Annexure D.

16. The Plaintiff claims total damages of Rs. 32,00,000/- (comprising Rs. 20,00,000/- refund and Rs. 12,00,000/- consequential damages) along with interest at 18% per annum from the date of breach until realization.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

18. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

19. The cause of action first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module as per the agreed deadline, thereby breaching the contract and causing the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue. The Defendant has not compensated the Plaintiff despite legal notice, and the Plaintiff's right to claim damages therefore subsists.

20. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

21. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

22. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at 18% per annum from the date of breach (01.05.2024) until the date of institution of the suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount, under Section 34 CPC.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{DAMAGES_AMOUNT}}/- as damages for breach of contract;
    (b) Award pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of institution of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual and direct financial loss
     Amount claimed: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-
     Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
     Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexur
